exercises.title: Lattice Builder
exercises.description: Create and visualize surface code lattices.
tasks: Generate L×L square lattice coordinates
Identify vertex, edge, and face elements
Create adjacency structures (edge-vertex, edge-face)
Visualize the lattice with ASCII art
Count elements and verify Euler formula

exercises.title: Boundary Identifier
exercises.description: Identify and classify boundaries in planar surface codes.
tasks: Mark smooth vs rough boundary edges
Identify boundary stabilizers (3-body terms)
Compute which logical operators can terminate where
Handle various aspect ratios (L1 × L2)
Visualize boundary types with different markers
hints: Smooth: edges on top/bottom for standard orientation

exercises.title: Chain and Cycle Analyzer
exercises.description: Analyze error chains and classify them topologically.
tasks: Input: set of edges representing error chain
Compute boundary (syndrome locations)
Determine if chain is open, closed, or spanning
Classify: trivial cycle, non-trivial cycle, or open chain
Identify which logical operator class a spanning chain belongs to
hints: Boundary: vertices with odd edge count
Non-trivial: closed but not boundary of region
Use graph algorithms to detect cycles

exercises.title: Topological Code Designer
exercises.description: Design surface codes with different topologies.
tasks: Implement planar code (1 logical qubit)
Implement toric code (2 logical qubits)
Compute k from Euler characteristic
Identify all independent logical operators
Verify logical operators commute with all stabilizers
hints: Toric: periodic boundaries in both directions
Logical X and Z for each logical qubit
